notes.md-73-**Constraints**:
/Users/arivero/phys3/notes.md-74-  - rs = 2N (squark balance)
/Users/arivero/phys3/notes.md-75-  - r(r+1)/2 = 2N (neutral scalar balance)
/Users/arivero/phys3/notes.md-76-  - r² + s² - 1 = 4N (neutrino sector with R-handed ν)
notes.md-77-**Solution**: N=3 with r=3 (down-type light), s=2 (up-type light).
notes.md:78:**Critical insight**: Asymptotic freedom upper bound (2n_f < 33) enforces uniqueness.
notes.md-79-**Group theory**: SU(5) ⊃ SU(3)×SU(2) decomposition: 24=(1,1)+(3,1)+(2,3)+(2,3̄)+(1,8); 15=(3,1)+(2,3)+(1,6).

/Users/arivero/phys3/sources/susy_generations.md-110-We can call this idea the **sBootstrap**, because if the scalar sector can produce itself from the fermion sector, and then the fermion sector can be produced from the scalar sector with the susy generator, the SSM matter bootstraps itself. /Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-63-\end{equation}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-64-so that we get one sextet of charge -1/3 and another sextet of charge +2/3. We call this
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-65-reproduction of the original charges a ``supersymmetric Bootstrap'', or {\em sBootstrap}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-66-for short, because it is possible to interpret the $SU(3)$ piece as coming from 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-67-three particles similar to quarks $d,s,b$ and the $SU(2)$ piece similar to quarks $u,c$. If we
tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex:68:pursue this interpretation, the uniqueness is more appealing: with more of three generations,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-69-not all of them are used in the flavour symmetry, and the number of extra particles
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-70-makes the scheme a lot uglier, adding more exotic squarks and sleptons to the bag.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-71-
tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex:72:This symmetry was found some years ago in \cite{Rivero:2005if} and the uniqueness is discussed with
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-73-more detail there, but even with three generations it had a 
tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-74-severe handicap: the $15$ multiplet
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-75-has three extra scalars not in the standard model. Fortunately, they were different from
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-76-the other scalars in the sense that, being a odd number, it was not possible to arrange them in
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios.tex-77-Dirac supermultiplets; then this chirality was expected to be an advantage
--

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-65-so that we get one sextet of charge -1/3 and another sextet of charge +2/3. We call this
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-66-reproduction of the original charges a ``supersymmetric Bootstrap'', or {\em sBootstrap}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-67-for short, because it is possible to interpret the $SU(3)$ piece as coming from 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-68-three particles similar to quarks $d,s,b$ and the $SU(2)$ piece similar to quarks $u,c$. If we
tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex:69:pursue this interpretation, the uniqueness is more appealing: with more of three generations,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-70-not all of them are used in the flavour symmetry, and the number of extra particles
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-71-makes the scheme a lot uglier, adding more exotic squarks and sleptons to the bag. Note
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-72-that also the same interpretation, with the same ``quarks'', applies to the 24 decomposition
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-73-on sleptons above.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-74-
tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex:75:This symmetry was found some years ago in \cite{Rivero:2005if} and the uniqueness is discussed with
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-76-more detail there, but even with three generations it had a 
tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-77-severe handicap: the $15$ multiplet
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-78-has three extra scalars not in the standard model. Fortunately, they were different from
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-79-the other scalars in the sense that, being a odd number, it was not possible to arrange them in
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/arivero/arivero-old/Documents/cuatrotercios2.tex-80-Dirac supermultiplets; then this chirality was expected to be an advantage

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex-160-hash are calculated.  If the hash table entry for that hash value is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex-161-not a null value, the second-level check is invoked.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex-162-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex-164-starting with the entry pointed to by the hash table entry, looking
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex-165-for an entry whose 32-bit rolling checksum matches the current value.
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex:166:The scan terminates when it reaches an entry whose 16-bit hash
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex-167-differs.  If this search finds a match, the third-level check is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex-168-invoked.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/doc/packages/rsync/tech_report.tex-171-current offset in the file and comparing it with the strong checksum

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/texlive/texlive-es/texlive-es.tex-1284-(\url{http://pages.uoregon.edu/koch/texshop}). En otros sistemas de
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/texlive/texlive-es/texlive-es.tex-1285-Unix, es tu decisión escoger un editor. Por supuesto que hay muchas
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/texlive/texlive-es/texlive-es.tex-1286-opciones disponibles, algunas de las cuales aparecen en la próxima

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/luatex/base/luatex-tex.tex:1512:\LUA\ level. The library provides a basic scanner infrastructure that can be used
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/luatex/base/luatex-tex.tex-1513-to write macros that accept a wide range of arguments. This interface is on
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/luatex/base/luatex-tex.tex-1514-purpose kept general and as performance is quite ok one can build additional
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/luatex/base/luatex-tex.tex-1515-parsers without too much overhead. It's up to macro package writers to see how
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/luatex/base/luatex-tex.tex-1516-they can benefit from this as the main principle behind \LUATEX\ is to provide a

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/luatex/base/luatex-enhancements.tex-324-tokens, contrary to all \TEX\ commands. So formally speaking its expansion is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/luatex/base/luatex-enhancements.tex-325-null, but it places material on a pseudo-file to be immediately read by \TEX, as

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1084-A property list is used to store the variables, organised by size \textendash{}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1085-the number of tokens composing an extended variable. This enables the sorting
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1086-by size needed for the determination of the overall coefficients of variables
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1087-by removing them in turn from the algebraic part of the expression. That
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1088-process is conducted in the routine \texttt{\textbackslash{}\_\_diffco\_eval\_vars:NN}.
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex:1089:The variables are recorded only on the first scan through the order specification
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1090-expression. This is the function of the boolean \texttt{\textbackslash{}l\_\_diffco\_vars\_noted\_bool}
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1091-which is set in \texttt{\textbackslash{}\_\_diffco\_eval\_vars:NN}. Evaluation
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1092-of the numeric parts of expressions is provided by \texttt{\textbackslash{}\_\_diffco\_eval\_nos:N}.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1093-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeff.tex-1094-\section{Summary of main commands}

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-481-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-482-Table~\ref{tab:Revised-input-output} lists the transitions. I've denoted
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-483-the previous state by $S_{-}$, the present state by $S$, and the next
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-484-state by $S_{+}$. The final row of the table is intended: do nothing if
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-485-we meet a multiplicative token when in a multiplicative state. The first
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex:486:scan through an order specification (to split it into numeric and algebraic
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-487-parts) may introduce a {*} token (rows 8 and 15). We don't want to introduce
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-488-a second such token in the recursive determination of the coefficients
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-489-of variables. Hence row 21: do nothing. Also, if in the order specification
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-490-we have something like $2^{3}*3^{2}$ (since \texttt{2\textasciicircum{}33\textasciicircum{}2}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-491-looks weird), we don't want the manually inserted {*} to cause an error
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-593-the expression. We are working through our expression $\mathbf{E}$ from
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-594-the left, token by token, and have no foreknowledge of what the parenthesised
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-595-expression contains, whether algebraic terms only or numeric terms only
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-596-or some combination of both. Hence the need to prepare for both. The system
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-597-shifts to a signed state \strong{s} with $T=+$, exactly the same as when
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex:598:beginning to scan $\mathbf{E}$. After all, the parenthesised expression
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-599-is an expression in itself.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-600-\item Row 2. This is to allow nested parentheses like )). It shouldn't arise
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-601-otherwise. Because of rows 4 and 5, the first right parenthesis puts the
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-602-system into a signed state. The current term will be $T=+$, but we ignore
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/diffcoeff/diffcoeffx.tex-603-it and store only a right parenthesis in both numeric and algebraic parts.
--

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/texlive/texlive-de/texlive-de-html.tex-2961-Intern nutzt \LaTeXe\ jetzt Unicode-basierte TeX-engine Konfiguration (welche Zeichen sind Buchstaben, Benamung der TeX primitives, etc.), die bisher Teil von \TeX\ Live waren. /Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-432-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-433-\subsection{Expressions}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-434-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-435-\eTeX\ introduces the notion of expressions of type number, dimen, glue, or
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-436-muglue, that can be used whenever a quantity of that type is needed. Such
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex:437:expressions are evaluated by \eTeX's scanning mechanism; they are initiated
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-438-by one of the commands \|\numexpr|, \|\dimexpr|, \|\glueexpr|, or \|\muexpr|
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-439-(determining the type~$t$) and optionally terminated by one \|\relax| (that
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex:440:will be absorbed by the scanning mechanism). An expression consists of one
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-441-or more terms of the same type to be added or subtracted; a term of type~$t$
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-442-consists of a factor of that type, optionally multiplied and\slash or
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-443-divided by numeric factors; finally a factor of type~$t$ is either a
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-444-parenthesized subexpression or a quantity (number, etc.) of that type.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-445-Thus, the conditional
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-528-The difference is that the current category codes are ignored and all
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-529-characters on that line (including an endline character) are converted to
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-530-character tokens with category 12 (`other'), except that the character
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-531-code~32 gets category 10 (`space').
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-532-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex:533:The command \|\scantokens{...}| absorbs a list of unexpanded tokens,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-534-converts it into a character string that is treated as if it were an
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-535-external file, and starts to read from this `pseudo-file'. A rather
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-536-similar effect can be achieved by the commands
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-537-\begin{verbatim}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-538-   \toks0={...}
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-542-   \input file
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-543-\end{verbatim}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-544-In particular every occurrence of the current newline character is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-545-interpreted as start of a new line, and input characters will be converted
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-546-into tokens as usual.
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex:547:The \|\scantokens| command is, however, expandable and does not use token
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-548-registers, write streams, or external files. Furthermore the conversion from
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-549-\TeX's internal ASCII codes to external characters and back to ASCII codes
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-550-is skipped. Finally the current context (traceback) shown, e.g., as part
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-551-of an error message continues beyond an input line from a pseudo-file until
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-552-an input line from a real file (or the terminal) is found.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-553-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-554-When \eTeX's input mechanism attempts to read beyond the end of an \|\input|
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex:555:file or \|\scantokens| pseudo-file, and before checking for `runaway'
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-556-conditions and closing the file, it will first read a list of tokens that
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-557-has been predefined by the command \|\everyeof={|\<token list>\|}|.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/etex/base/etex_man.tex-558-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2506-manner --- if something is boring, then there are too many other interesting
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2507-things for the reader to do than to plow on until the bitter end, 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2508-assuming that he
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex:2509:even started to read seriously after an initial scan. The other aspect is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2510-the manner in which the content is displayed. Or, in other words, 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2511-the \emph{typography}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2512-of the book, which is the subject of this chapter.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2513-

--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2849-covered with type. The same proportions that are useful for the shape of a 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2850-page are also useful for the shape of the typeblock. This does not mean, 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2851-though, that the proportions of the page and the typeblock should be the same. 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/fonts/memdesign/memdesign.tex-2852-For instance, a square typeblock on a square page is inherently dull.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-41-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:42:The \TEX\ scanner roughly provides the following sub-scanners that are used to
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-43-implement primitives: keyword, token, token list, dimension, glue and integer.
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:44:Deep down there are specific variants for scanning, for instance, font dimensions
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-45-and special numbers.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-46-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-47-A token is a unit of input, and one or more characters are turned into a token.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-48-How a character is interpreted is determined by its current catcode. For instance
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-49-a backslash is normally tagged as `escape character' which means that it starts a
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:50:control sequence: a macro name or primitive. This means that once it is scanned a
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-51-macro name travels as one token through the system. Take this:
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-52-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-53-\starttyping
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-54-\def\foo#1{\scratchcounter=123#1\relax}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-55-\stoptyping
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-56-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:57:Here \TEX\ scans \type {\def} and turns it into a token. This particular token
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:58:triggers a specific branch in the scanner. First a name is scanned with
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:59:optionally an argument specification. Then the body is scanned and the macro is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-60-stored in memory. Because \type {\scratchcounter}, \type
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-61-{\relax} and \type {#1} are
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-62-turned into tokens, this body has 7~tokens.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-63-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-64-When the macro \type {\foo} is referenced the body gets expanded which here means
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:65:that the scanner will scan for an argument first and uses that in the
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:66:replacement. So, the scanner switches between different states. Sometimes tokens
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-67-are just collected and stored, in other cases they get expanded immediately into
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-68-some action.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-69-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-70-\stopsection
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-71-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-73-

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-75-instance:

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-83-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-86-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-88-\stoptyping

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-91-write additional ones as you can just grab tokens, interpret them and act

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-93-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:94:The \type {scan_int} function picks up a number. This can also be a counter, a
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-95-named (math) character or a numeric expression. In \TEX, numbers are integers;
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:96:floating|-|point is not supported naturally. With \type {scan_dimen} a dimension
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-97-is grabbed, where a dimen is either a number (float) followed by a unit, a dimen
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-98-register or a dimen expression (internally, all become integers). Of course
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-99-internal quantities are also okay. There are two optional arguments, the first
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-100-indicating that we accept a filler as unit, while the second indicates that math
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:101:units are expected. When an integer or dimension is scanned, tokens are expanded
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:102:till the input is a valid number or dimension. The \type {scan_glue} function

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-284-\NC \type{\uwrap}      \NC \TokTest \uwrap      \NC \NR
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-285-\stoptabulate
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-286-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:287:Because scanners look ahead the following happens: when an open brace is seen (or
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:288:any character marked as left brace) the scanner picks up tokens and expands them
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:289:unless they are protected; so, effectively, it scans as if the body of an \type
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:290:{\edef} is scanned. However, when the next token is a control sequence it will be
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-291-expanded first to see if there is a left brace, so there we get the full
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-292-expansion. In practice this is convenient behaviour because the braced variant
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-293-permits us to pick up meanings honouring protection. Of course this is all a side
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:294:effect of how \TEX\ scans.\footnote {This lookahead expansion can sometimes give
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-295-unexpected side effects because often \TEX\ pushes back a token when a condition
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:296:is not met. For instance when it scans a number, scanning stops when no digits
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:297:are seen but the scanner has to look at the next (expanded) token in order to
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-298-come to that conclusion. In the process it will, for instance, expand
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-299-conditionals. This means that intermediate catcode changes will not be effective
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-300-(or applied) to already-seen tokens that were pushed back into the input. This
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-301-also happens with, for instance, \cs {futurelet}.}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-302-
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-308-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-309-\startsection[title=Considerations]
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-310-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-311-Performance|-|wise there is not much difference between these methods. With some
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-312-effort you can make the second approach faster than the first but in practice you
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:313:will not notice much gain. So, the main motivation for using the scanner is that
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-314-it provides a more \TEX|-|ified interface. When playing with the initial version
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:315:of the scanners I did some tests with performance|-|sensitive \CONTEXT\ calls and
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-316-the difference was measurable (positive) but deciding if and when to use the
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:317:scanner approach was not easy. Sometimes embedded \LUA\ code looks better, and
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-318-sometimes \TEX\ code. Eventually we will end up with a mix. Here are some
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-319-considerations:
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-320-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-321-\startitemize
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-322-\startitem
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-325-\startitem
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-326-    In the pure \LUA\ case the whole argument is tokenized by \TEX\ and then
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-327-    converted to a string that gets compiled by \LUA\ and executed.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-328-\stopitem
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-329-\startitem
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:330:    When the scan happens in \LUA\ there are extra calls to functions but
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:331:    scanning still happens in \TEX; some token to string conversion is avoided
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-332-    and compilation can be more efficient.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-333-\stopitem
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-334-\startitem
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-335-    When data comes from external files, parsing with \LUA\ is in most cases more
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-336-    efficient than parsing by \TEX .
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-337-\stopitem
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-338-\startitem
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-339-    A macro package like \CONTEXT\ wraps functionality in macros and is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-340-    controlled by key|/|value specifications. There is often no benefit in terms
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:341:    of performance when delegating to the mentioned scanners.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-342-\stopitem
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-343-\stopitemize
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-344-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-345-Another consideration is that when using macros, parameters are often passed
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-346-between \type {{}}:
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-379-So in the end you don't gain much here apart from the fact that the fake
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-380-primitive can be made more clever and accept optional arguments. But such new
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-381-features are often hidden for the user who uses more high|-|level wrappers.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-382-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-383-When you code in pure \TEX\ and want to grab a number directly you need to test
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:384:for the braced case; when you use the \LUA\ scanner method you still need to test
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:385:for braces. The scanners are consistent with the way \TEX\ works. Of course you
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-386-can write helpers that do some checking for braces in \LUA, so there are no real
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-387-limitations, but it adds some overhead (and maybe also confusion).
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-388-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-389-One way to speed up the call is to use the \type {\luafunction} primitive in
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-390-combinations with predefined functions and although both mechanisms can benefit
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:391:from this, the scanner approach gets more out of that as this method cannot be
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-392-used with regular function calls that get arguments. In (rather low level) \LUA\
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-393-it looks like this:
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-394-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-395-\starttyping
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-396-luafunctions[1] = function()
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:397:  local a token.scan_string()
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:398:  local n token.scan_int()
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:399:  local b token.scan_string()
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-400-  -- whatever --
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-401-end

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex:562:There are many ways to use the scanners and each has its charm. We will look at
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-563-some alternatives from the perspective of performance. The timings are more meant

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/still/still-tokens.tex-575-Thus, \type {1.23} is valid, as are \type {0x1234} and \type {12.12E4}.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/csquotes/csquotes.tex-176-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/csquotes/csquotes.tex-177-This option controls the maximum nesting level of quotations. By default, this package supports two levels of quotations, outer and inner ones, and issues an error if quotations are nested more deeply. Alternatively, it can reuse the outer and inner quotes on further quotation levels. This alternative behavior is enabled implicitly when increasing the value of the \opt{maxlevel} option. The minimum is~\texttt{2}.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/csquotes/csquotes.tex-178-

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/csquotes/csquotes.tex:181:This option controls whether the quotation commands scan ahead for trailing punctuation marks. See \secref{bas:txt:reg,bas:blk:reg,int:txt:reg,int:blk:reg} for details. Also see \cmd{DeclareAutoPunct} in \secref{cfg:dap} and \secref{cfg:aux} on how to configure and use the punctuation look-ahead feature. The short form \opt{autopunct} is equivalent to \kvopt{autopunct}{true}.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-elements.tex-302-\begin{command}{\ifbeamertemplateempty\marg{beamer template name}\marg{executed if empty}\marg{executed otherwise}}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-elements.tex-303-  This command checks whether a template is defined and set to a non-empty text. If the text is empty or the template is not defined at all, \meta{executed if empty} is executed. Otherwise, \meta{executed otherwise} is executed.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-elements.tex-304-\end{command}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-elements.tex-305-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-elements.tex-306-\begin{command}{\expandbeamertemplate\marg{beamer template name}}
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-elements.tex:307:  This command does the same as |\usebeamertemplate|\marg{beamer template name}. The difference is that this command performs a direct expansion and does not scan for a star. This is important inside, for example, an |\edef|. If you don't know the difference between |\def| and |\edef|, you won't need this command.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-elements.tex-308-\end{command}

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-455-\beamer\ extends the syntax of \LaTeX's standard command |\newcommand|:
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-456-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-457-\begin{command}{\newcommand\declare{|<>|}\marg{command name}\oarg{argument number}\oarg{default optional value}\marg{text}}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-458-  Declares the new command named \meta{command name}. The \meta{text} should contain the body of this command and it may contain occurrences of parameters like |#|\meta{number}. Here \meta{number} may be between 1 and $\mbox{\meta{argument number}}+1$. The additionally allowed argument is the overlay specification.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-459-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex:460:[Omitted long matching line]
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-461-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-462-  If the \meta{default optional value} is provided, the first argument of \meta{command name} is optional. If no optional argument is specified in square brackets, the \meta{default optional value} is used.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-463-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-overlays.tex-464-  \example

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex-1107-\end{inventory}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex-1108-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex-1109-%\point Introduction
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex-1110-\section{Introduction}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex-1111-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex:1112:\TeX's input processor scans input lines from a file or terminal, and
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex-1113-makes tokens out of the characters.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex-1114-The input processor can be viewed as
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex-1115-a simple finite state automaton with three internal states; 
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/plain/texbytopic/TeXbyTopic.tex:1116:depending on the state its scanning behaviour may differ.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-143-  \item
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-144-    \declare{|fragile|\opt{|=singleslide|}} tells \beamer\ that the frame contents is ``fragile.'' This means that the frame contains text that is not ``interpreted as usual.'' For example, this applies to verbatim text, which is, obviously, interpreted somewhat differently from normal text.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-145-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-146-    If a frame contains fragile text, different internal mechanisms are used to typeset the frame to ensure that inside the frame the character codes can be reset. The price of switching to another internal mechanism is that either you cannot use overlays or an external file needs to be written and read back (which is not always desirable).
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-147-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex:148:    In detail, the following happens when this option is given for normal (pdf)\LaTeX: The contents of the frame is scanned and then written to a special file named \meta{jobname}|.vrb| or, if a label has been assigned to the frame, \meta{jobname}|.|\meta{current frame number}|.vrb|. Then, the frame is started anew and the content of this file is read back. Since, upon reading of a file, the character codes can be modified, this allows you to use both verbatim text and overlays.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-149-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-150-    To determine the end of the frame, the following rule is used: The first occurrence of a single line containing exactly |\end{|\meta{frame environment name}|}| ends the frame. The \meta{environment name} is normally |frame|, but it can be changed using the |environment| option. This special rule is needed since the frame contents is, after all, not interpreted when it is gathered.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-151-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-152-    You can also add the optional information |=singleslide|. This tells \beamer\ that the frame contains only a single slide. In this case, the frame contents is \emph{not} written to a special file, but interpreted directly, which is ``faster and cleaner.''
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-153-  \item
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex:154:    \declare{|environment=|\meta{frame environment name}}. This option is useful only in conjunction with the |fragile| option (but it is not used for |fragile=singleslide|, only for the plain |fragile|). The \meta{frame environment name} is used to determine the end of the scanning when gathering the frame contents. Normally, the frame ends when a line reading |\end{frame}| is reached. However, if you use |\begin{frame}| inside another environment, you need to use this option:
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/beamer/doc/beamerug-frames.tex-155-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-386-If the <first_token> is a control sequence then the <byte_sequence>
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-387-will be converted to this control sequence of the ``one token'' 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-388-form at input processor level. It means that the 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-389-token processor never changes this control sequence. 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-390-The token processor stays in
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex:391:middle line state after this control sequence is scanned.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-392-If {\catcode`\<=12 "\mubyteout<2"} then the output to the "\write"
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-393-files is not converted back to the <byte_sequence> and
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-394-the control sequence is expanded as usual.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-395-If "\mubyteout>=2" then the control sequence declared by "\mubyte" is

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-417-The "\mubyte", "\endmubyte" primitives work very similar as a

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-419-the <first_token> is not expanded and that this token can be followed
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-420-by <one_optional_space> (after expansion). The <byte_sequence> is

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-422-sequence than "\endmubyte" occurs during this process then the error
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/enctex/encdoc-e.tex-423-message is printed:

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/about/about-metafun.tex-29-strings back into the \TEX\ input stream. A complication is that we have to be
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/about/about-metafun.tex-30-able to operate under different so called catcode regimes: the meaning of
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/about/about-metafun.tex-31-characters can differ per regime. We also have to deal with line endings in
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/about/about-metafun.tex-32-special ways as they relate to paragraphs and such. In \METAPOST\ we don't have
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/context/sources/general/manuals/about/about-metafun.tex-33-that complication so getting back input into the \METAPOST\ input, we can do so

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/biblatex/biblatex.tex-8775-\kvopt{all}{true} means that the child entry inherits all fields from the parent, unless a more specific inheritance rule has been set up with \cmd{DeclareDataInheritance}. If an inheritance rule is defined for a field, data inheritance is controlled by that rule. \kvopt{all}{false} means that no data is inherited from the parent by default and each field to be inherited requires an explicit inheritance rule set up with \cmd{DeclareDataInheritance}. The package default is \kvopt{all}{true}.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/biblatex/biblatex.tex-8776-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/biblatex/biblatex.tex-8777-\boolitem[false]{override} Whether or not to overwrite target fields with source fields if both are defined. This applies both to automatic inheritance and to explicit inheritance rules. The package default is \kvopt{override}{false}, \ie existing fields of the child entry are not overwritten.

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/biblatex/biblatex.tex:9646:Note that \cmd{mkcomprange} is executed first, using \cmd{mkpageprefix} as post-processor. Also note that the \prm{postpro} argument is wrapped in an additional pair of braces. This is only required in this particular case to prevent \latex's optional argument scanner from getting confused by the nested brackets. The starred version of this command differs from the regular one in the way the \prm{postpro} argument is applied to a list of values. For example:

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4517-(a) No. (b) No.  In other words, a blank line will produce a \piif{par} if
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4518-and only if endline characters are present and have catcode 5. It is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4519-interesting to note that two consecutive endline characters are not
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4520-translated simply to \piif{par}, but to \meta{space}\piif{par}.  (The space will
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4521-disappear in some circumstances, e.g., after a control word, according
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4522:to TeX's normal scanning rules.) This is the reason (or at least one
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4523-reason) that a \piif{par} operation must perform an implicit \cmd{\unskip}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4524-operation. There was also a recent post to \pfile{comp.text.tex} by Donald
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4525-Arseneau\index{Arseneau, Donald} to point out the problem with someone's

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4744-I'd say Donald Arseneau\index{Arseneau, Donald} deserves credit for 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4745-the best analysis,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4746-including an accurate survey of brace-stripping problems. Nearly
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4747-everyone, including myself, had missed a lurking flaw of that kind in
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4748-the first submitted version of their solution. Another good idea of
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4749:Donald's that caught my fancy was to use TeX's built-in scanning
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4750-procedures for \meta{optional space} to strip the leading space in
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4751-\cmd{\trimspaces}. I managed to work that into my own best solution, much to
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4752-my satisfaction.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4753-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4754-Peter Schmitt\index{Schmitt, Peter} came up with perhaps the most 
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4910-dealing with braces (character tokens with catcode 1 or 2) because a
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4911-lone brace cannot be passed as a macro argument. A recent posting by
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4912-\'Eamonn McManus to comp.text.tex on a different sort of problem
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4913-showed that the braces can indeed be dealt with, it's just not easy.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4914-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4915:The second, simpler approach is to use TeX's scanning of delimited
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4916:macro arguments to scan for the ending space and discard it. If you
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4917:merely scan for a space token, however, you end up scanning through
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4918-the given text `word' by `word' (word = sequence of non-space
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4919-characters or brace-delimited groups) instead of token by token, which
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4920-is perhaps if anything even more awkward than the first method above,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4921-since you still must deal with brace complications.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4922-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4923:The key refinement, therefore, is to scan for a pair of tokens: a
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4924-space token and some well-chosen bizarre token that can't possibly
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4925:occur in the scanned text. If you put the bizarre token at the end of
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4926-the text, and if the text has a trailing space, then TeX's delimiter
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4927-matching will match at that point and not before, because the earlier
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4928-occurrences of space don't have the requisite other member of the pair.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4929-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4930-Next consider the possibility that the trailing space is absent: TeX
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4931:will keep on scanning ahead for the pair \meta{space}\meta{bizarre} until either
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4932-it finds them or it decides to give up and signal a `Runaway
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4933-argument?' error. So you must add a stop pair to catch the runaway
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4934-argument possibility: a second instance of the bizarre token, preceded
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4935-by a space. If TeX doesn't find a match at the first bizarre token, it
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4936-will at the second one.
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4957-In other words, one way to remove a leading space would be
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4958-\begin{lcode}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4959-  \expandafter\def\expandafter\foo\expandafter{\ifdim0pt=0pt\foo \fi}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4960-\end{lcode}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4961-The \cmd{\expandafter}'s would cause the \piif{ifdim} to be executed first.
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4962:Execution of the \piif{ifdim} will not terminate until the scanning of the
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4963-second `0pt' is finished; therefore TeX will start expanding \cmd{\foo} as
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4964:part of the scanning of the `0pt'. Then if a space is the first thing
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4965-inside the expansion of \cmd{\foo}, it will be removed by TeX as denoting
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4966-the end of the dimension. Otherwise the first non-space token will
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:4967:terminate the dimension scanning and will be left in place (well, I am
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4968-glossing over the problem of an expandable token at the beginning of
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4969-\cmd{\foo}, which can be handled by further refinements).
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4970-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4971-Notice that as written the trailing \piif{fi} will be included in the
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-4972-redefinition of \cmd{\foo}. No problem---just rewrite it with the \piif{fi}

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5737-register like \cmd{\z@} but the additional clause `minus 2pt' fills out the

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5741-component. Unlike the height, depth, and width components of a \cmd{\vrule} or
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5742-\cmd{\hrule}, the components of a glue value have a required order and each
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5743-part can only occur once.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5744-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5758-\end{lcode}
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5759-(7): F --- Without the factor 2 in front of \cmd{\topskip}, the test would
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5760-be True: \cmd{\topskip} is a glue register so TeX would copy each component of
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5761-\cmd{\topskip} to the corresponding component of \cmd{\baselineskip}; then, having plus
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:5762:and minus components already in hand, TeX would not scan ahead for
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5763-`plus' or `minus'. However, a preceding factor for a glue register
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5764-causes TeX to use only the first component of the glue register,
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:5765:multiplied by the given factor, which means that additional scanning is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5766-then attempted for possible stretch or shrink components.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5767-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5768-\begin{lcode}

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5770-\end{lcode}

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:5794:comes along, before it finishes scanning the skip amount. So the
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:5795:\piif{ifvmode} test is false. The \piif{ifdim} test is also false because scanning
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5796-is not yet complete (TeX is looking ahead for a plus or minus component)
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5797-so the glue has not yet been entered into the horizontal list, so it is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-5798-not accessible to \cmd{\lastskip}.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7196-            user-level command; for each user-level command, only two 
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7197-            command-specific macros are needed: the top-level one invoked by 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7198-            the user, and the internal one that handles all the arguments. 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7199-            By contrast, the standard LaTeX method of handling multiple 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7200-            options requires a separate command-specific macro for each step 
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:7201:            of the argument scanning. 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7202-\item The number of optional arguments is quasi-limited. The number 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7203-            of default values that you give in a command's definition 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7204-            becomes an upper limit on the number of arguments that will be 
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex:7205:            scanned for. And if you supply twenty default values, the code 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7206-            that ends up handling them will have to be more than a simple 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7207-            TeX macro since macro arguments only go up to 9. 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7208-\item Commands defined with this method can be nested, because the 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7209-            delimiters for the optional arguments are regular curly braces \verb?{ }?,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7210-            not square brackets [ ].

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/around-the-bend/AroundTheBend.tex-7283-% will stop as soon as $d$ arguments have been read, if not before.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/custom.tex-20-\begin{lscommand}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/custom.tex-21-\ci{dum}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/custom.tex-22-\end{lscommand}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/custom.tex-23-\end{example}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/custom.tex-24-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/custom.tex:25:En este ejemplo, estoy usando tanto un nuevo entorno llamado\\ \ei{lscommand}, que es responsable de dibujar la caja alrededor de la orden, y una nueva orden llamada \ci{ci}, que compone el nombre de la orden y hace la correspondiente entrada en el índice.  Puede comprobarlo buscando la orden \ci{dum} en el índice al final del libro, donde pude encontrar una entrada para \ci{dum}, apuntando a cada página donde he mencionado la orden \ci{dum}.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/custom.tex-26-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/custom.tex-27-Si alguna vez decido que no me gusta que las órdenes se compongan en una caja, puedo simplemente cambiar la definición del entorno \texttt{lscommand} para crear un nuevo aspecto.  Esto es mucho más fácil que ir por todo el documento localizando todos los lugares en que he usado comandos \LaTeX{} genéricos para dibujar una caja alrededor de una palabra.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lshort-spanish/fuente/src/spec.tex-336-Hasta ahora todo buenas noticias.  Las malas noticias son que los gráficos en formato \EPSi{} no funcionan con  pdf\LaTeX{}.  Si no incluye una extensión de \filenomo{} en la orden \ci{includegraphics}, \pai{graphicx} buscará un \filenomo{} adecuado, en función de lo establecido en la opción del \emph{controlador}.  Para \texttt{pdftex} esto significa los formatos \texttt{.png}, \texttt{.pdf}, \texttt{.jpg} y \texttt{.mps} (\MP\index{metapost@\MP}), pero \emph{no} \texttt{.eps}.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-330-\pfg\ 3.0 supports the embedded \cs{tex} commands found in previous release of
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-331-\pfg. Used properly, this is a powerful tool, but it has been deprecated
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-332-somewhat because of its reliance on a pre-processing step. Unlike previous
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-361-	  past the other graphics in your EPS file. As a result, they can modify
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-362-	  the EPS bounding box in an undesired way. This problem can be mitigated by
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-363-	  reducing the font size of the \cs{tex} string, since this does not affect
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-364-	  the size of its \pfg\ replacement.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-365-\item The \cs{tex} command is not supported in compressed PostScript files.
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex:366:\item The \TeX\ engine must scan the PostScript file for these strings,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-367-	  which can add to the processing time of your document. (To be honest,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-368-	  we have yet to encounter a case where this is a significant concern.)
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex:369:\item \emph{Important!} Whenever a file is scanned by \pfg,
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-370-	  it generates a file with the name \cs{jobname}\fname{.pfg}, where \cs{jobname}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-371-	  is the base name of the master \LaTeX\ file. It will overwrite, without
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-372-	  warning, any file with that name.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-373-\end{itemize}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-374-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-375-This feature is no longer enabled automatically, except in \LaTeX\ 2.09
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex-376-compatibility mode. So, for \LaTeXe\ documents, you must activate it in one of

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/psfrag/pfgguide.tex:382:	  exited; or, you can turn it off explicity with a call to \cs{psfragscanoff}.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/pgf/text-en/pgfmanual-en-pgffor.tex-70-  Let us move on to a more complicated setting. The first

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/pgf/text-en/pgfmanual-en-pgffor.tex-72-  curly braces. If the |\foreach| statement does not encounter an
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/pgf/text-en/pgfmanual-en-pgffor.tex:73:  opening brace, it will instead scan everything up to the next
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/pgf/text-en/pgfmanual-en-pgffor.tex-74-  semicolon and use this as \meta{commands}. This is most useful in

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/pgf/text-en/pgfmanual-en-pgffor.tex-76-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/pgf/text-en/pgfmanual-en-pgffor.tex-77-\begin{codeexample}[]

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/latex/lualatex-doc-de/lualatex-doc-DE.tex-717-an. Aber auf einer niedrigeren Ebene werden nicht-ASCII Zeichen in diesem Fall
