/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2775-This primitive is used for reading a font map file consisting of
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2776-one or more font map lines. The name of the map file is given in
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2777-the \Something{map spec} together with an optional leading modifier
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2778-character. If no \type{\pdfmapfile} primitive is given, the default map
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2779-file \filename{pdftex.map} will be read by \PDFTEX. There is a companion
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex:2780:primitive \type{\pdfmapline} that allows to scan single map lines;
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2781-its map line argument has the same syntax as the map lines from a map
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2782-file. Both primitives can be used concurrently. The \type{\pdfmapfile}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2783-primitive is fast for reading external bulk font map information (many
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2784-map lines collected in a map file), whereas the \type{\pdfmapline} allows
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2785-to put the font map information for individual \TeX\ fonts right into
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2786-the \TeX\ source or a style file. In any case the map line information
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex:2787:is scanned by \PDFTEX, and in the most common case the data are put into
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2788-a fresh internal map entry data structure, which is then consulted once
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2789-a font is called.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2790-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2791-Normally there is no need for the \PDFTEX\ user to bother about the
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-2792-\type{\pdfmapfile} or \type{\pdfmapline} primitives, as the main \TEX\

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4456-embedding of images with simple transparency. The \PNG\ format does not

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4458-(this often can be replaced by four component \JPEG\ in high quality or
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4459-lossless compression mode). Photos in \PNG\ format have a rather weak
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4477-embedding photos.  It's not recommended for crisp images from synthetic
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4478-sources with a limited amount of colors.  Both \JFIF\ and \EXIF\ are
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4479-supported for additional information.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4480-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4481-The \JBIGTWO\ format works only for bitonal (black and white) pixel
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex:4482:images like scanned line and text documents, but for these it has
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4483-typically a much higher compression ratio than the other two pixel
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4484-image formats. The \JBIGTWO\ format is part of the \PDF\ standard since
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4485-version 1.5; native \JBIGTWO\ image inclusion is available in \PDFTEX\
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4486-since version 1.40.0. A \JBIGTWO\ file might contain many images, which
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/manual/pdftex-t.tex-4487-gives an even better compression ratio than with a single image per file,
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/pstricks-add/pstricks-add-doc.tex-3653-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/pstricks-add/pstricks-add-doc.tex-3654-\begin{BDef}

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-172-\subsection{Description}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-173-[Omitted long context line]
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-174-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-175-On appelle \guill{chaîne de tokens} une suite de tokens quelconques, sachant qu'aucune supposition n'a été faite quant à leur nature, mis à part que dans les chaînes de tokens, les accolades doivent être équilibrées et que les tokens de catcode 6 et 14 (habituellement \verb|%| et \verb|#|) n'y sont pas admis. Tous les autres tokens, y compris \verbinline|\par|, sont \emph{à priori} permis dans n'importe quel ordre, quelque soit le code qu'ils représentent.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-176-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex:177:[Omitted long matching line]
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-178-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-179-[Omitted long context line]
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-180-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-181-Comme les macros manipulent des chaînes de tokens, il peut arriver aux utilisateurs avancés de rencontrer des problèmes de \guill{catcodes\footnote{Codes de catégories, en français.}} conduisant à des comportements inattendus. Ces effets indésirables peuvent être contrôlés. Consulter en particulier le chapitre sur les catcodes des arguments page~\pageref{macrosetoilees}.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1170-\subsubsection{Les macros {\ttfamily \textbackslash fullexpandarg}, {\ttfamily \textbackslash expandarg} et {\ttfamily \textbackslash noexpandarg}}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1171-La macro \verbinline|\fullexpandarg| est appelée par défaut, ce qui fait que certains arguments (en \textcolor{violet}{violet} dans la liste ci dessous) transmis aux macros sont développés le plus possible (pour cela, un \verb|\edef| est utilisé). Ce mode de développement maximal permet dans la plupart des cas d'éviter d'utiliser des chaînes d'\verb|\expandafter|. Le code en est souvent allégé.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1172-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1173-On peut interdire le développement de ces arguments (et ainsi revenir au comportement normal de \TeX) en invoquant \verbinline|\noexpandarg| ou \verbinline|\normalexpandarg| qui sont synonymes.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1174-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex:1175:Il existe enfin un autre mode de développement de ces arguments que l'on appelle avec \verbinline|\expandarg|. Dans ce cas, le \textbf{premier token} de ces arguments est développé \emph{une fois} avant que la macro ne soit appelée. Si l'argument contient plus d'un token, les tokens qui suivent le premier ne sont pas développés (on peut contourner cette volontaire limitation et utiliser la macro \verbinline|\StrExpand|, voir page~\pageref{scancs}).\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1176-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1177-Les commandes \verbinline|\fullexpandarg|, \verbinline|\noexpandarg|, \verbinline|\normalexpandarg| et \verbinline|\expandarg| peuvent être appelées à tout moment dans le code et fonctionnent comme des bascules. On peut rendre leur portée locale dans un groupe.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1178-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1179-On peut également mémoriser le mode de développement en cours avec \verbinline|\saveexpandmode|, le modifier par la suite et revenir à la situation lors de la sauvegarde avec \verbinline|\restoreexpandmode|.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1180-

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex:1663:\label{scancs}
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1664-La macro \verbinline|\StrExpand| développe les tokens de la chaîne passée en argument. Voici sa syntaxe :\par\nobreak\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1665-\verbinline|\StrExpand|\arguC{nombre}\ARGU{chaine}\ARGU{nom}\smallskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1666-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1667-Le \argu{nombre} vaut 1 par défaut et représente le nombre de développement(s) que doit subir la \argu{chaine} de tokens. Le \argu{nom} est le nom d'une séquence de contrôle à laquelle est assigné le résultat, une fois que tous les tokens aient été développé le nombre de fois demandé.\medskip

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1716-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1717-Le développement de ce qui se trouve à l'intérieur des groupes est \emph{indépendant} du mode d'exploration des groupes : cette macro possède ses propres commandes pour développer ce qui est dans les groupes ou pas. Par défaut, les tokens se trouvant à l'intérieur des groupes sont développés, mais on peut demander à ce que ce développement ne se fasse pas en invoquant \verbinline-\noexpandingroups- et revenir au comportement par defaut par \verbinline-\expandingroups-.\bigskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1718-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex:1719:On peut détokeniser le résultat obtenu par la macro \verbinline-\StrExpand- avec la macro \verbinline-\scancs- dont la syntaxe est :\par\nobreak\medskip
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex:1720:\verbinline|\scancs|\arguC{nombre}\ARGU{nom}\ARGU{chaine}\par\nobreak\smallskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1721-Le \argu{nombre} vaut 1 par défaut et représente le nombre de développement(s) que doit subir chaque token constituant la \argu{chaine}. Le \argu{nom} est le nom d'une séquence de contrôle à laquelle est assigné le résultat, une fois que tous les tokens aient été développés le nombre de fois demandé et ensuite détokénisés.\smallskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1722-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex:1723:\verbinline-\scancs-  a été conservée pour garder une compatibilité avec des précédentes versions de \Xstring. Pour les mêmes raisons, sa syntaxe n'est pas cohérente avec la syntaxe des autres macros. Cette macro, devenue triviale, prend le résultat de \verbinline|\StrExpand| et lui applique simplement un \verbinline|\detokenize|.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1724-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1726-Avec le verbatim, certaines difficultés surviennent lorsque l'on se trouve à l'intérieur de la définition d'une macro, c'est-à-dire entre les accolades suivant un \verb|\def\macro| ou un \verb|\newcommand\macro|.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1727-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1728-Pour les mêmes raison qu'il est interdit d'employer la commande \verb|\verb| à l'intérieur de la définition d'une macro, les arguments  verb du type |\argu{caractères}| sont également interdits, ce qui disqualifie la macro \verbinline|\verbtocs|. Il faut donc observer la règle suivante :\par\medskip
--
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1739-	\item les signes \verb|%| sont interdits;
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_fr.tex-1740-	\item une espace est ajoutée après chaque séquence de contrôle.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-175-\subsection{Description}
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-176-This extension\footnote{This extension does not require \LaTeX{} and can be compiled with Plain $\varepsilon$-\TeX{}.} provides macros and tests operating on "strings of tokens", as other programmation languages have. They provides the usual strings operations, such as: test if a string contains another, begins or ends with another, extractions of strings, calculation of the position of a substring, of the number of occurrences, etc.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-177-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-178-A "string of tokens" is a list of tokens of any nature, except that braces must be balanced and tokens catcode 6 and 14 (usualy \verb|%| and \verb|#|) are not allowed. Apart from this, any token is allowed (including \verbinline|\par|), in any order in the list, whatever be the resulting code.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-179-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex:180:[Omitted long matching line]
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-181-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-182-As the arguments may contain any token, advanced users could have problems with catcodes leading to unexpected behaviours. These behaviours can be controlled: read page~\pageref{macrosetoilees}.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-183-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-184-Certainly, other packages exist (for example \href{http://www.ctan.org/tex-archive/macros/latex/contrib/substr/}{\nolinkurl{substr}} and \href{http://www.ctan.org/tex-archive/macros/latex/contrib/stringstrings/}{\nolinkurl{stringstrings}}), but as well as differences on features, they do not take into account occurrences so I found them too limited and difficult to use for programming.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-185-

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1194-\subsubsection{The commands {\ttfamily \textbackslash fullexpandarg}, {\ttfamily \textbackslash expandarg} and {\ttfamily \textbackslash noexpandarg}}
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1195-The command \verbinline|\fullexpandarg| is called by default, so all the arguments are fully expanded (an \verbinline|\edef| is used) before the the macro works on them. In most of the cases, this expansion mode avoids chains of \verbinline|\expandafter| and allows lighter code.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1196-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1197-Of course, the expansion of argument can be canceled to find back the usual behaviour of \TeX{} with the commands \verbinline|\noexpandarg| or \verbinline|\normalexpandarg|.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1198-
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex:1199:Another expansion mode can be called with \verbinline|\expandarg|. In this case, the \textbf{first token} of each argument is expanded \emph{one time} while all other tokens are left unchanged (if you want the expansion of all tokens one time, you should call the macro \verbinline|\StrExpand|, see page~\pageref{scancs}).\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1200-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1201-The commands \verbinline|\fullexpandarg|, \verbinline|\noexpandarg|, \verbinline|\normalexpandarg| and \verbinline|\expandarg| can be called at any moment in the code; they behave as "switches" and they can be locally used in a group.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1202-
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1203-It is possible to save the expansion mode with \verbinline|\saveexpandmode|, then modify this expansion mode and come back to the situation when it was saved with \verbinline|\restoreexpandmode|.\medskip
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1204-

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/xstring/xstring_doc_en.tex-1744-Expansion inside groups is \emph{independant} of the exploration mode: this macro has its own command to expand or not what is inside the groups. By default, tokens inside groups are expanded, but this can be changed with the macro \verbinline-\noexpandingroups-. The default behaviour can be recoverd with \verbinline-\expandingroups-.\bigskip

It is also possible to use \verbinline|\tokenize| to transform the final result (which is generaly \texte{10,11,12}) into control sequences. See example using these macros at the end of this manual, page~\pageref{exemples}.\medskip

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2923-a general solution) for comments, since the matter being skipped is
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex:2924:nevertheless scanned by \TeX{}, not always as you would expect.  The

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2926-not be a problem in \emph{today's} job, but could return to bite you

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2929-\begin{quote}

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2932-consider what happens if we
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2933-use \verb+\iftrue+ -- a surprise
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2934-\fi
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2935-\end{verbatim}

tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex:2937:The \csx{iftrue} is spotted by \TeX{} as it scans, ignoring the
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2938-\csx{verb} command; so the \csx{iffalse} isn't terminated by the
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2939-following \csx{fi}.  Also, \csx{gobble} is pretty inefficient at
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2940-consuming anything non-trivial, since all the matter to be skipped is
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/generic/FAQ-en/faq-how-do-i.tex-2941-copied to the argument stack before being ignored.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1023-%D so that vertical lines come out an integer number of pixels
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1024-%D wide in device space. The \type{0 y ...} form does the same
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1025-%D for paths that are {\em primarily horizontal}. The reason
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1026-%D why I did this is Knuth insists on getting exactly the
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1027-%D widths \TEX\ intends for the horizontal and vertical rules
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex:1028:%D in \type{btex...etex} output. (Note that PostScript scan
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1029-%D conversion rules cause a horizontal or vertical line of
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1030-%D integer width $n$ in device space to come out $n+1$ pixels
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1031-%D wide, regardless of the phase relative to the pixel grid.)
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1032-%D \stopnarrower
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/doc/pdftex/samplepdftex/supp-pdf.tex-1033-%D

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-26-%  The macro reads the headres first and then reads the lines 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-27-%  with the text. The contents of each item can be accessed 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-28-%  by full expanable macro \e after each line is read. More preciselly, 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-29-%  \e[header] expands to the body of the appropriate item.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-32-%
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-33-%  Next line of the table is read after \lineaction, the \e macros have 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-34-%  a new meaning (items from this next line) and the \lineaction is executed
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-35-%  again. This is repeated until last line of the input table is reached.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex-36-%  Moreower, the \linenum register is available, where the number of the
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scanbase.tex:37:%  last scanned line is stored.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-34-%  Next line of the table is read after \lineaction, the \e macros have 
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-35-%  a new meaning (items from this next line) and the \lineaction is executed
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-36-%  again. This is repeated until last line of the input table is reached.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-37-%  Moreower, the \linenum register is available, where the number of the
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex:38:%  last scanned line is stored.

/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-67-%  its own (default) macro which prints all items from one line
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-68-%  into the one paragraph in comprimend form (you can try this).
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-69-%  
tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex:70:%  The \scanbase macro opens the TeX group then runs \beginhook
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-71-%  then reads headers, then reads the lines ans processes \linecation
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-72-%  repeatedly, then runs \endhook and finally closes the group.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-73-%  Default values for \beginhook and \endhook is \relax but user
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-74-%  can define something else.
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-75-%
/Users/arivero/phys3/tmp/oldmachine/srv/usr_share/texmf/tex/generic/olsak-misc/scancsv.tex-76-%  The user can redefine the \separator and \obklopeni (surrounding char)
